behavior_summary: This script implements NPC Kilandra's examination text, reactive behaviors (when attacked or when the player steals), and a long-running wandering/interaction AI that runs while the player is nearby.

U8P_KILANDRA::look provides context-sensitive description text: it checks Npc::isDead() and the globals kilandraName and kilandraJob to choose an appropriate bark (corpse text, full name+job, name-only, job-only, or generic ‘woman’).
U8P_KILANDRA::enterFastArea activates Kilandra's AI by clearing reference slots and spawning the AI interpreter U8P_KILANDRA::0495.
U8P_KILANDRA::leaveFastArea clears references and, if Kilandra is alive, may reposition her randomly (inferred via urandom + Npc::teleport) as a cleanup/reposition behavior.
U8P_KILANDRA::schedule chooses a scheduled routine using FREE::28F9 flags and spawns METHOD::133F with selected parameters to set up timed behaviors.

U8P_KILANDRA::cast reacts to being attacked/cast on: it checks target via Npc::getTarget and if the Avatar is aggressor (and Kilandra is alive) it summons help (CALGUARD::0080), barks an alarm, and uses a timer helper (FREE::1C19) for cooldown/repeats.

U8P_KILANDRA::AvatarStoleSomething responds to theft by clearing refs, turning to the stolen item (Item::getDirToItem + METHOD::091C), chastising the player with a bark, and triggering active behavior/schedule.

U8P_KILANDRA::0495 is the main AI loop: it interprets a script of token frames (Item::getFrame), pathfinds to scripted points, spawns short action handlers (U8P_KILANDRA::05AC), and uses suspend/timers to pace movement.
U8P_KILANDRA::05AC handles small interactions reached by the AI: conditional dialog (first-meet vs repeat using kilandraMet), facing targets, fishing-related lines, and other short actions.
notable_control_flow: Look handler branches first on Npc::isDead() and then on globals kilandraName and kilandraJob to select wording.
enterFastArea -> spawn(U8P_KILANDRA::0495) to begin active behavior; leaveFastArea -> clear/free refs and optionally teleport when alive.
AI interpreter (U8P_KILANDRA::0495) loops through script tokens (Item::getFrame), calling Npc::pathfind and spawning U8P_KILANDRA::05AC at markers; it uses suspend to yield between steps.
Reactive handlers (cast, AvatarStoleSomething) clear references, call orientation helpers, and spawn guard or schedule/timer helpers to escalate or defer responses.
schedule queries several FREE::28F9 flags to select one of multiple METHOD::133F parameter sets, enabling different timed behaviors to be launched.
open_questions: Exact text variants and their string IDs are not present; what are the literal bark strings for each kilandraName / kilandraJob combination?
Precise meanings of FREE::28F9 flag indices used by schedule(): what runtime states do those bits represent?
What exact script/token data set does U8P_KILANDRA::0495 iterate (shapes/frames mapping to world positions) and where is it stored?
Does leaveFastArea teleport use map-safe validation or can it place Kilandra in inaccessible locations (teleport coordinates inferred but not fully documented)?
Are there additional global flags written by this script (none observed) or by spawned METHOD::133F instances that affect Kilandra's long-term state?

main_function.name: U8P_KILANDRA::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the descriptive/examination text when a player looks at Kilandra. It chooses phrasing based on whether Kilandra is dead and on global flags that control name and job variants.
entry_conditions: Event 0: invoked when a player (pid) examines / looks at the NPC Kilandra

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::look
function_map.role: Produce context-sensitive examination text (bark) for the NPC based on death state and global name/job flags.

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::schedule
function_map.role: Select and spawn a scheduled behavior (METHOD::133F) by querying FREE::28F9 flags and setting schedule parameter bytes.

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Trigger when the avatar enters Kilandra's fast trigger region: clear refs and spawn the long-running AI (U8P_KILANDRA::0495).

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Trigger on leaving the fast area: free/clear refs and, if alive, reposition Kilandra (inferred teleport to random coords).

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::cast
function_map.role: React to being cast/attacked: check target, summon guards, bark alarm, and use a timer/cooldown pattern (FREE::1C19).

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: Handle theft by the Avatar: reset refs, turn to the stolen item, chastise the player, and trigger active behaviors/schedule.

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::0495
function_map.role: Long-running AI/pathing interpreter that follows a script, pathfinds, spawns short action handlers, and controls pacing with suspends.

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::05AC
function_map.role: Short action handler invoked by the AI: handles greetings (first-meet vs repeat via kilandraMet), dialog, facing, and small context actions.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Engine helper used by U8P_KILANDRA::look and other handlers to determine whether Kilandra is dead and should suppress actions/dialog.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine primitive to display/speak text to a pid; primary mechanism used for all dialog and descriptive strings.

internal_calls.purpose: Activate the NPC's active AI pathing/interaction loop when the player enters the fast area.
Spawn short action handler when AI reaches a scripted interaction marker.
Trigger scheduled/active behavior after a theft reaction (inferred from spawn patterns).
Ensure active AI behavior runs after responding to theft (inferred spawn on reaction).

external_calls.purpose: Check whether Kilandra is dead to select corpse vs living description text.
Send the chosen descriptive string to the viewing player (pid).
Query flag/availability bits used to select which schedule parameters to use.
Spawn the selected scheduled behavior with chosen parameters.
Clear stored reference/state slots before starting active AI.
Free/clear stored references on leaving the fast area.
Choose random coordinates for a teleport reposition when leaving the fast area (inferred).
Reposition Kilandra to chosen coordinates (inferred behavior when leaving area).
Determine the NPC's current target to check if the Avatar is the aggressor.
Call for guards/allies when attacked by the Avatar.
Bark an alarm/chastising line when assaulted.
Compute direction toward the stolen item so Kilandra can face it.
Spawn a turn/rotation method so Kilandra faces the stolen item.
Request pathfinding toward scripted coordinates during the AI loop.
Read script token/frame values that drive AI behavior decisions.
Retrieve player name to personalize dialog lines.
Spawn a timer/delayed helper to implement cooldown or repeated checks.

reads.name: kilandraName (global[0107])
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether Kilandra's proper name is included in U8P_KILANDRA::look and dialog strings (selects name-vs-generic variants).

reads.name: kilandraJob (global[0108])
reads.likely_effect: Selects job-specific wording in U8P_KILANDRA::look and dialog (e.g., 'fisherwoman' vs generic 'woman').

reads.name: kilandraMet (global[0106])
reads.likely_effect: Indicates whether the player has met Kilandra before; used to choose first-meet greeting vs repeat greeting in U8P_KILANDRA::05AC.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements NPC Kilandra's examination text, reactive behaviors (when attacked or when the player steals), and a long-running wandering/interaction AI that runs while the player is nearby.
